# services.linux-enable-ir-emitter

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.linux-enable-ir-emitter`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.linux-enable-ir-emitter.device` | `string` | IR camera device to depend on. For example, for `/dev/video2` the value would be `video2`. Find this with the command {command}`realpath /dev/v4l/by-path/<generated-driver-name>`. |
| `services.linux-enable-ir-emitter.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable IR emitter hardware. Designed to be used with the Howdy facial authentication. After enabling the service, configure the emitter with `sudo linux-enable-ir-emitter configure`. |
| `services.linux-enable-ir-emitter.package` | `package` | Package to use for the Linux Enable IR Emitter service. |
